Name: Meals on Wheels
Description: Meal delivery service for seniors who cannot cook at home. Hot meals delivered to your door daily by volunteers.
Eligibility: Seniors 60+ who are homebound or have mobility limitations

Name: Better at Home Program
Description: Non-medical home support such as light cleaning grocery shopping and friendly visits. Helps seniors stay independent at home.

Name: HandyDART
Description: Accessible door-to-door transit service for people with mobility challenges. Book rides for medical appointments shopping and activities. Bus rides transit rides transportation handydart.
Eligibility: People with disabilities or mobility limitations including seniors

Name: SAFER Program
Description: Rental assistance program for low-income seniors. Helps pay rent so seniors can afford to stay in their homes.

Name: Seniors Health Clinic
Description: Medical services specialized for senior patients including wellness checkups chronic disease management and referrals to specialists.

Name: Adult Day Program
Description: Day programs with supervision and social activities for seniors. Includes recreation meals and peer connection.
Eligibility: Seniors with care needs or isolation

Name: Community Meals Program
Description: Provides free or low-cost group meals for seniors in a community setting. Hot lunch served Monday to Friday.

Name: Seniors Information Line
Description: Information and referral services for seniors. Call 211 to find any service you need in BC.

Name: Home Care Services
Description: In-home care including personal care bathing dressing and nursing visits for eligible seniors.
Eligibility: Eligible seniors assessed by health authority

Name: Income Assistance Program
Description: Financial support for low-income individuals and seniors. Covers basic living expenses.

Name: Senior Peer Support
Description: Volunteer-based emotional and social support for isolated seniors. Friendly phone calls and in-person visits.

Name: Transportation Access Program
Description: Subsidized transportation services for seniors to medical appointments grocery stores and community events. Rides bus transit handydart.

Name: Assisted Living Services
Description: Housing with support services for daily living including meals housekeeping and personal care.
Eligibility: Seniors who need daily support but not full nursing care

Name: Food Bank Program
Description: Provides free food hampers for individuals and families facing financial hardship. No questions asked.

Name: Mental Health Support Line
Description: Phone-based mental health support counseling and crisis line available 24 hours 7 days a week.

Name: Caregiver Support Program
Description: Support resources education and respite for people caring for seniors or family members with disabilities.
Eligibility: Caregivers of seniors or people with disabilities

Name: Seniors Legal Assistance
Description: Free legal advice and support for seniors facing housing issues elder abuse or financial exploitation.

Name: Emergency Response Program
Description: Personal emergency alert systems for seniors living alone. Press button to get help anytime anywhere.

Name: Housing Registry Service
Description: Helps individuals find affordable housing options in Greater Victoria. Connects seniors with subsidized and supportive housing.

Name: Community Recreation Program
Description: Recreational programs fitness classes and social activities for seniors. Aquafit yoga walking clubs and crafts.

Name: HandyDART Booking Service
Description: Book accessible transportation rides for medical appointments and errands. Door-to-door bus service for seniors and people with mobility challenges. Transit rides handydart bus.

Name: Tax Assistance for Seniors
Description: Free tax filing help for seniors with simple tax returns. Volunteers help complete your income tax forms.
Eligibility: Seniors with low income and simple tax situations

Name: Grocery Delivery Program
Description: Free grocery delivery and shopping assistance for seniors who cannot leave home. Delivers nutritious food directly to your door.

Name: Wheelchair and Walker Loan
Description: Free short-term loan of wheelchairs walkers canes and other mobility equipment for seniors and people recovering from injury.
Eligibility: Seniors and people with mobility challenges

Name: Senior Fitness Classes
Description: Low-impact fitness and exercise classes designed for seniors including chair yoga gentle stretching and aquafit.

Name: Alzheimers and Dementia Support
Description: Support groups education and resources for people with dementia and their families and caregivers.
Eligibility: People with dementia and their caregivers

Name: Dental Care for Seniors
Description: Subsidized dental care and oral health services for low-income seniors including checkups cleaning and extractions.

Name: Housing Repair Program
Description: Home repair and modification grants for low-income seniors to stay safely in their homes. Includes grab bars ramps and accessibility modifications.

Name: Volunteer Driver Program
Description: Free volunteer-driven rides to medical appointments for seniors who cannot drive. Transit alternative bus alternative rides.

Name: Energy Assistance Program
Description: Help paying energy bills for low-income seniors. Covers electricity heating and utility costs.

Name: Adult Learning Centre
Description: Adult education programs for seniors including computer literacy English language and life skills workshops.
Eligibility: Adults and seniors of all ages

Name: Friendly Visitor Program
Description: Trained volunteers visit isolated seniors at home or in care facilities for companionship and social connection.

Name: Senior Abuse Support Line
Description: Confidential support and resources for seniors experiencing abuse neglect or financial exploitation.
Eligibility: Seniors experiencing any form of abuse

Name: Public Transit Pass
Description: Subsidized bus pass for low-income seniors to use public transit across Greater Victoria. BC transit bus rides transit.

Name: Respite Care Services
Description: Temporary care for seniors to give their family caregivers a break. In-home or residential short-term stays.
Eligibility: Seniors whose caregivers need a break
